prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Polyolefin encapsulant with 7 MPa elastic modulus showed no chemical reaction with perovskite after 1000h at 85 degrees C
2. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Stiff Surlyn with 394 MPa elastic modulus caused PSC delamination by pulling up on ITO layer creating moisture ingress pathways
3. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Commercial encapsulant elastic moduli: EVA 10 MPa, Surlyn 394 MPa, 3M polyolefin 9.1 MPa, CVF 20 MPa, ENLIGHT 7 MPa
4. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Butyl rubber edge seal has two orders of magnitude lower WVTR than UV-curable epoxies and polymeric encapsulants
5. [GOLD] Source: Investigating the Crosslinking, Degradation, and Adhesion Be | Statement: Adhesion energy Gc decreased after 5000 h of hot-dry (90 degrees C, ~1% RH) and hot-humid (90 degrees C, 60% RH) aging on cell/encapsulant/glass coupo
